name: La Rinconada and Las Pajanosas Circular
name_original: Desde La Rinconada a Las Pajanosas – circular por Alcalá del Río
description: This 30.8-mile road cycling route from La Rinconada to Las Pajanosas via Alcalá del Río offers a moderate challenge through rural Andalusia.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around La Rinconada are primarily situated in the flat to gently rolling plains near the Guadalquivir River in Andalusia, Spain. The landscape features agricultural fields, olive groves, and occasional small hills, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Routes often follow paved country roads and dedicated bike paths, offering a mix of open stretches and shaded sections. Best road cycling routes around La Rinconada The most popular road cycling route is La Rinconada and Las Pajanosas Circular, a 30.8 miles (49.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 6 minutes to complete. This moderate route features gentle ascents through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Guillena loop from La Rinconada, an easy 30.3 miles (48.8 km) path. This route offers a relatively flat ride through agricultural areas, suitable for a relaxed pace. Local road cyclists also love the Camino de Santiago – Section through the Sierra de Seville loop from El Cáñamo, a 71.0 miles (114.3 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Seville, often completed in about 5 hours 7 minutes. Road cycling around La Rinconada is defined by agricultural plains, riverine landscapes, and small hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in La Rinconada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore La Rinconada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around La Rinconada?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes around La Rinconada, offering a diverse range of options for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in La Rinconada?
A: The terrain around La Rinconada primarily features flat to gently rolling plains near the Guadalquivir River. You'll cycle through agricultural fields, olive groves, and occasional small hills. Routes often follow paved country roads and dedicated bike paths, providing a mix of open stretches and shaded sections.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy ride?
A: Yes, La Rinconada offers several easy road cycling routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39560697" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guillena loop from La Rinconada&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 48.8 km path that offers a relatively flat ride through agricultural areas, perfect for a relaxed pace. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20155693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Little trains loop from El Cáñamo&lt;/a&gt;, covering about 45.7 km.

Q: Are there more challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20199339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Santiago – Section through the Sierra de Seville loop from El Cáñamo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 114.3 km trail. This route leads through the Sierra de Seville and involves significant elevation changes, often taking over 5 hours to complete.

Q: What are some popular circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Many routes around La Rinconada are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994055668/la-rinconada-and-las-pajanosas-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rinconada and Las Pajanosas Circular&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 49.6 km trail featuring gentle ascents through the local countryside. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39560697" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guillena loop from La Rinconada&lt;/a&gt; is another well-liked circular option.

Q: What landmarks or natural features can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass through scenic agricultural plains and riverine landscapes. While cycling, you might encounter iconic landmarks like the Alamillo Bridge and the Tower of Gold in the wider Seville area. You can also explore natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7365230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monumental Carob Tree&lt;/a&gt; or the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5271187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;María Luisa Park&lt;/a&gt;, and historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1055590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Real Alcázar of Seville&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2456715" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marchenilla Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options?
A: Yes, the region offers routes suitable for families, particularly those with a relaxed pace and relatively flat terrain. The easy-rated routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39560697" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guillena loop from La Rinconada&lt;/a&gt;, are good choices for a family outing, as they traverse agricultural areas with minimal elevation gain.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in La Rinconada?
A: Given its location in Andalusia, Spain, the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling. Temperatures are milder, and the landscape is often lush. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable, while winters are typically mild but can be cooler and wetter.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in La Rinconada?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which includes flat plains and gentle hills, making it suitable for different fitness levels. The scenic rides through agricultural fields and along riverine landscapes are also frequently highlighted as enjoyable aspects of the experience.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by cafes or places to stop for refreshments?
A: While specific cafes are not detailed for every route, many routes pass through or near small towns and villages where you can typically find local establishments for refreshments. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31869893" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fairground – Bar La Farola loop from El Cáñamo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 53.2 km route that suggests a stop at Bar La Farola, indicating opportunities for breaks along the way.

Q: How long do road cycling routes typically take in La Rinconada?
A: The duration of routes varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39560697" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guillena loop from La Rinconada&lt;/a&gt; (48.8 km) can take around 2 hours. Moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994055668/la-rinconada-and-las-pajanosas-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rinconada and Las Pajanosas Circular&lt;/a&gt; (49.6 km), also average about 2 hours. Longer, more difficult routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20199339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Santiago – Section through the Sierra de Seville loop from El Cáñamo&lt;/a&gt; (114.3 km), can take over 5 hours.
tips: What to see when you are in Guillena - the most important monuments: Parroquia de Ntra. Sra. de la Granada, Plaza de España, Plaza de Toros, Palacio de Parladé. Guillena is part of the Camino de Santiago (Vía de la Plata between Seville and Astorga).

Ideal place for a short stop on the way to Seville. Las Pajanosas has been serving the traveler for centuries with its typical sale and passing services that are currently convenient for the cyclists who frequent this road.
The Rodríguez de la Borbolla Bridge, popularly known as the Old Bridge. Inaugurated in 1923, it was the first bridge between La Algaba and the capital and replaced the old boat crossing.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This moderate road cycling route is designed to be completed in approximately 2 hours and 6 minutes, though your actual time may vary depending on your pace and stops along the way.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner cyclists?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as 'moderate' and requires a good level of fitness. While it's not overly demanding, beginners might find the nearly 50 km distance and around 277 meters of elevation gain challenging. It's best suited for regular cyclists.

faqs.question: Where does the cycling route start, and what are the parking and public transport options?
faqs.answer: The route typically starts in La Rinconada. You can find parking in the town. A convenient aspect of this route is that La Rinconada is also accessible by public transport, making it easy to reach the starting point without a car.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is characterized by mostly paved surfaces, making it ideal for road bikes. The roads in this part of Andalusia have generally been improved and resurfaced, so you can expect a smooth and enjoyable ride for the majority of the journey. Sections like the N-630 near Las Pajanosas are known for consistent pavement and light traffic.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: As you cycle, you'll experience varied and captivating scenery, including rural landscapes and charming small-town environments connecting La Rinconada, Alcalá del Río, and Las Pajanosas. Near Alcalá del Río, you might encounter natural settings shaped by rivers, forests, and hillsides. A notable landmark along the way is the Rodríguez de la Borbolla Bridge (Old Bridge of La Algaba).

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Andalusia generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring (March-May) and Autumn (September-November) are often ideal, with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, so if cycling then, it's best to start early in the morning to avoid the midday heat.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public road cycling route in Andalusia.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route primarily on public roads. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, it's not specifically designed as a dog-friendly trail. If you bring a dog, ensure it is safely secured in a bike trailer or carrier, and always follow local regulations regarding pets in towns and public areas.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route briefly overlap with other paths. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Ruta del Agua, Mtb Vía de la Plata, and the Vía de la Plata hiking - 01 Sevilla to Merida, among others.

faqs.question: What amenities or services are available in the towns along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through towns like La Rinconada, Alcalá del Río, and Las Pajanosas. These settlements offer opportunities to stop at traditional bars, cafes, or shops for refreshments and supplies. Guillena is also a highlight along the way where you might find services.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a road cycling trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given it's a moderate road cycling route, essential items include a well-maintained road bike, helmet, appropriate cycling attire, water bottles, snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and sun protection. Don't forget your phone for navigation and emergencies.

waypoints.name: Rodríguez de la Borbolla Bridge (Old Bridge of La Algaba)
